'''Lasko''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] settler from [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]] who migrated to [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. 

==Biography==
Lasko's [[marriage/Legends|wife]] was [[Death/Legends|killed]] during in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], when an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] force under [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Lord Jerec]] [[Subjugation of Sulon|subjugated]] [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]]; the [[gender/Legends|woman]] had joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] forces that [[Battle of the G-Tap|defended]] the [[Geothermal tap|G-Tap]] against the Imperials.<ref name="JK">''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]''</ref>

At some time earlier or later than that, Lasko joined the Sulonese settlers who had migrated to [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] to flee the wrath of the Empire.<ref name="Dark Forces: Rebel Agent">''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''</ref> There he started a new life, married again and in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]] had a baby.<ref name="JK" />

However his hatred for the Empire had not subsided, and he was to meet again with Jerec's minions: six months after the birth of his baby, the planet had been rediscovered by the [[Imperial Remnant]]. An off-world [[assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttle]] landed in their town, and Lasko was among the curious bystanders who gathered near the [[Hangar|landing pad]]. Seeing the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] emerge from inside, led by three [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]], he wondered openly why they don't just open fire against them.<ref name="JK" />

Hearing this, and sensing his hatred through the Force, the Dark Jedi leader, [[Sariss]], picked him from the crowd and [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Choke|Force-choked]] him nearly to death. As he pumped on the ground, Sariss released him from her hold. Panicked, Lasko was lost into the intimidated throng, and once past it, he started running towards his [[skimmer/Legends|skimmer]], and flew to the badlands and his family who hoped would be safe.<ref name="JK" />

Sariss took pleasure with the fear of the citizens, teaching them her lesson. Shortly after, the town [[Battle of Fort Nowhere|was destroyed]]. Lasko with other survivors fled away to the badlands; they followed [[Grif Grawley]], who knew the area, and hid in an [[Unidentified Ruusan temple|ancient temple]].<ref name="JK" />

Not long after, an [[Moldy Crow/Legends|unknown ship]], following the directions transmitted by Grif, reached the temple courtyard; Lasko, along with [[Kimber]] and [[Luther Pardy]] were standing armed beside Grif as he welcomed the pilots, one of whom was an old acquaintance from Sulon, [[Kyle Katarn]], and took their weapons. The three of them were ordered by Grif to cover the newly arrived ship with a netting in order to hide it from Imperial observations, and enter the temple. Inside, Lasko listened carefully Katarn's story, from his defection years ago to the [[Search for the Valley of the Jedi|quest for the Valley of the Jedi]], which brought him back to their [[homeworld/Legends|home]], Sulon, and his encounter with some of the [[Seven Dark Jedi]]. Lasko understood they belonged to the same group he encountered earlier and found his story interesting and believable, and was ready to defend Katarn over Pardy's skepticism. He saw Katarn displaying his [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]] by [[Telekinesis/Legends|pulling]] [[Qu Rahn's lightsaber|his lightsaber]] from Luther's belt. Lasko felt hope knowing that a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi]] would face those [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]].<ref name="JK" />

==Behind the scenes==
It is not clear when Lasko migrated to Ruusan. In the ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' novella, [[Morgan Katarn]] took those settlers from Sulon some months ''before'' the [[Subjugation of Sulon]] and his own death, where Lasko's wife was also killed. This means that Lasko had followed Katarn to Ruusan while Lasko's wife chose to stay behind to Sulon. Another possibility is that Lasko left for Ruusan in some later date after the Subjugation of Sulon, joining the Sulonese settlers there, in some later migration which is not mentioned.

In the [[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|audio adaptation]] of ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' [[Louise Enyeart]], who voices [[Marie Peeno]], is also credited as "Lasko". Lasko's name is not heard in the dramatized dialogue. It is possible that "Lasko" is a woman who notices the shuttle with the Dark Jedi landing in Fort Nowhere, and then corrects a man next to her that he shouldn't call [[Boc Aseca]] a "wormhead" at his face, but "[[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]]".
<!-- The above assumption is based on the fact that there are not many characters speaking in the Fort Nowhere scene, and the female voice sounds similar to Peeno's voice heard later, also voiced by Lousie Enyeart, making possible that the unnamed character is Lasko. -->
